1→package validation
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"testing"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/iw2rmb/ploy/internal/arf/models"
     7→)
     8→
     9→func TestLLMModelValidator_ValidateLLMModel(t *testing.T) {
    10→	validator := NewLLMModelValidator()
    11→
    12→	tests := []struct {
    13→		name    string
    14→		model   *models.LLMModel
    15→		wantErr bool
    16→		errMsg  string
    17→	}{
    18→		{
    19→			name: "valid openai model",
    20→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    21→				ID:           "gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06",
    22→				Name:         "GPT-4o Mini",
    23→				Provider:     "openai",
    24→				Version:      "2024-08-06",
    25→				Capabilities: []string{"code", "analysis"},
    26→				Config:       map[string]string{"temperature": "0.7"},
    27→				MaxTokens:    128000,
    28→				CostPerToken: 0.00015,
    29→			},
    30→			wantErr: false,
    31→		},
    32→		{
    33→			name: "valid anthropic model",
    34→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    35→				ID:           "claude-3-sonnet@v1",
    36→				Name:         "Claude 3 Sonnet",
    37→				Provider:     "anthropic",
    38→				Version:      "v1",
    39→				Capabilities: []string{"code", "analysis", "reasoning"},
    40→				Config:       map[string]string{"top_p": "0.9"},
    41→				MaxTokens:    200000,
    42→				CostPerToken: 0.003,
    43→			},
    44→			wantErr: false,
    45→		},
    46→		{
    47→			name: "valid azure model",
    48→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    49→				ID:           "azure-gpt-4@v1",
    50→				Name:         "Azure GPT-4",
    51→				Provider:     "azure",
    52→				Version:      "v1",
    53→				Capabilities: []string{"code", "multimodal"},
    54→				Config:       map[string]string{"deployment_name": "gpt-4-deployment"},
    55→				MaxTokens:    8192,
    56→				CostPerToken: 0.03,
    57→			},
    58→			wantErr: false,
    59→		},
    60→		{
    61→			name: "nil model",
    62→			model: nil,
    63→			wantErr: true,
    64→			errMsg:  "model cannot be nil",
    65→		},
    66→		{
    67→			name: "model with short ID",
    68→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    69→				ID:           "ab",
    70→				Name:         "Test Model",
    71→				Provider:     "openai",
    72→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
    73→				MaxTokens:    1000,
    74→			},
    75→			wantErr: true,
    76→			errMsg:  "model ID too short",
    77→		},
    78→		{
    79→			name: "model with long ID",
    80→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    81→				ID:           "this-is-an-extremely-long-model-id-that-exceeds-the-maximum-allowed-length-for-model-identifiers-and-should-definitely-fail",
    82→				Name:         "Test Model",
    83→				Provider:     "openai",
    84→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
    85→				MaxTokens:    1000,
    86→			},
    87→			wantErr: true,
    88→			errMsg:  "model ID too long",
    89→		},
    90→		{
    91→			name: "model with invalid ID characters",
    92→			model: &models.LLMModel{
    93→				ID:           "invalid$model#id",
    94→				Name:         "Test Model",
    95→				Provider:     "openai",
    96→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
    97→				MaxTokens:    1000,
    98→			},
    99→			wantErr: true,
   100→			errMsg:  "invalid model ID format",
   101→		},
   102→		{
   103→			name: "azure model without deployment_name",
   104→			model: &models.LLMModel{
   105→				ID:           "azure-gpt-4@v1",
   106→				Name:         "Azure GPT-4",
   107→				Provider:     "azure",
   108→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   109→				Config:       map[string]string{"temperature": "0.7"},
   110→				MaxTokens:    8192,
   111→			},
   112→			wantErr: true,
   113→			errMsg:  "Azure models require 'deployment_name'",
   114→		},
   115→		{
   116→			name: "model with low token limit",
   117→			model: &models.LLMModel{
   118→				ID:           "small-model@v1",
   119→				Name:         "Small Model",
   120→				Provider:     "local",
   121→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   122→				MaxTokens:    500,
   123→			},
   124→			wantErr: true,
   125→			errMsg:  "max_tokens too low",
   126→		},
   127→		{
   128→			name: "openai model with high token limit",
   129→			model: &models.LLMModel{
   130→				ID:           "huge-model@v1",
   131→				Name:         "Huge Model",
   132→				Provider:     "openai",
   133→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   134→				MaxTokens:    2000000,
   135→			},
   136→			wantErr: true,
   137→			errMsg:  "max_tokens for OpenAI models cannot exceed",
   138→		},
   139→		{
   140→			name: "anthropic model with multimodal (not supported)",
   141→			model: &models.LLMModel{
   142→				ID:           "claude-3@v1",
   143→				Name:         "Claude 3",
   144→				Provider:     "anthropic",
   145→				Capabilities: []string{"multimodal"},
   146→				MaxTokens:    200000,
   147→			},
   148→			wantErr: false, // multimodal is now supported for anthropic
   149→		},
   150→		{
   151→			name: "local model with function_calling (not supported)",
   152→			model: &models.LLMModel{
   153→				ID:           "local-model@v1",
   154→				Name:         "Local Model",
   155→				Provider:     "local",
   156→				Capabilities: []string{"function_calling"},
   157→				MaxTokens:    8000,
   158→			},
   159→			wantErr: true,
   160→			errMsg:  "capability 'function_calling' not supported by provider 'local'",
   161→		},
   162→	}
   163→
   164→	for _, tt := range tests {
   165→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   166→			err := validator.ValidateLLMModel(tt.model)
   167→			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
   168→				t.Errorf("ValidateLLMModel() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   169→				return
   170→			}
   171→			if tt.wantErr && err != nil && tt.errMsg != "" {
   172→				if !containsString(err.Error(), tt.errMsg) {
   173→					t.Errorf("ValidateLLMModel() error = %v, want error containing %v", err, tt.errMsg)
   174→				}
   175→			}
   176→		})
   177→	}
   178→}
   179→
   180→func TestLLMModelValidator_ValidateModelUpdate(t *testing.T) {
   181→	validator := NewLLMModelValidator()
   182→
   183→	existingModel := &models.LLMModel{
   184→		ID:           "gpt-4@v1",
   185→		Name:         "GPT-4",
   186→		Provider:     "openai",
   187→		Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   188→		MaxTokens:    8192,
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	tests := []struct {
   192→		name    string
   193→		existing *models.LLMModel
   194→		updated  *models.LLMModel
   195→		wantErr bool
   196→		errMsg  string
   197→	}{
   198→		{
   199→			name:    "valid update",
   200→			existing: existingModel,
   201→			updated: &models.LLMModel{
   202→				ID:           "gpt-4@v1",
   203→				Name:         "GPT-4 Updated",
   204→				Provider:     "openai",
   205→				Capabilities: []string{"code", "analysis"},
   206→				MaxTokens:    8192,
   207→			},
   208→			wantErr: false,
   209→		},
   210→		{
   211→			name:     "nil existing model",
   212→			existing: nil,
   213→			updated:  existingModel,
   214→			wantErr:  true,
   215→			errMsg:   "existing model cannot be nil",
   216→		},
   217→		{
   218→			name:     "nil updated model",
   219→			existing: existingModel,
   220→			updated:  nil,
   221→			wantErr:  true,
   222→			errMsg:   "updated model cannot be nil",
   223→		},
   224→		{
   225→			name:    "changed ID",
   226→			existing: existingModel,
   227→			updated: &models.LLMModel{
   228→				ID:           "gpt-4@v2",
   229→				Name:         "GPT-4",
   230→				Provider:     "openai",
   231→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   232→				MaxTokens:    8192,
   233→			},
   234→			wantErr: true,
   235→			errMsg:  "model ID cannot be changed",
   236→		},
   237→		{
   238→			name:    "changed provider",
   239→			existing: existingModel,
   240→			updated: &models.LLMModel{
   241→				ID:           "gpt-4@v1",
   242→				Name:         "GPT-4",
   243→				Provider:     "anthropic",
   244→				Capabilities: []string{"code"},
   245→				MaxTokens:    8192,
   246→			},
   247→			wantErr: true,
   248→			errMsg:  "model provider cannot be changed",
   249→		},
   250→	}
   251→
   252→	for _, tt := range tests {
   253→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   254→			err := validator.ValidateModelUpdate(tt.existing, tt.updated)
   255→			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
   256→				t.Errorf("ValidateModelUpdate() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   257→				return
   258→			}
   259→			if tt.wantErr && err != nil && tt.errMsg != "" {
   260→				if !containsString(err.Error(), tt.errMsg) {
   261→					t.Errorf("ValidateModelUpdate() error = %v, want error containing %v", err, tt.errMsg)
   262→				}
   263→			}
   264→		})
   265→	}
   266→}
   267→
   268→func TestLLMModelValidator_validateProviderSpecificConfig(t *testing.T) {
   269→	validator := NewLLMModelValidator()
   270→
   271→	tests := []struct {
   272→		name     string
   273→		provider string
   274→		config   map[string]string
   275→		wantErr  bool
   276→		errMsg   string
   277→	}{
   278→		{
   279→			name:     "valid openai config",
   280→			provider: "openai",
   281→			config:   map[string]string{"temperature": "0.7", "api_base": "https://api.openai.com"},
   282→			wantErr:  false,
   283→		},
   284→		{
   285→			name:     "invalid openai config key",
   286→			provider: "openai",
   287→			config:   map[string]string{"invalid_key": "value"},
   288→			wantErr:  true,
   289→			errMsg:   "invalid OpenAI config key",
   290→		},
   291→		{
   292→			name:     "valid anthropic config",
   293→			provider: "anthropic",
   294→			config:   map[string]string{"temperature": "0.7", "top_k": "40"},
   295→			wantErr:  false,
   296→		},
   297→		{
   298→			name:     "invalid anthropic config key",
   299→			provider: "anthropic",
   300→			config:   map[string]string{"invalid_key": "value"},
   301→			wantErr:  true,
   302→			errMsg:   "invalid Anthropic config key",
   303→		},
   304→		{
   305→			name:     "valid azure config",
   306→			provider: "azure",
   307→			config:   map[string]string{"deployment_name": "gpt-4", "api_version": "2023-05-15"},
   308→			wantErr:  false,
   309→		},
   310→		{
   311→			name:     "azure config missing deployment_name",
   312→			provider: "azure",
   313→			config:   map[string]string{"api_version": "2023-05-15"},
   314→			wantErr:  true,
   315→			errMsg:   "Azure models require 'deployment_name'",
   316→		},
   317→		{
   318→			name:     "valid local config",
   319→			provider: "local",
   320→			config:   map[string]string{"endpoint": "http://localhost:8080", "timeout": "30s"},
   321→			wantErr:  false,
   322→		},
   323→		{
   324→			name:     "unknown provider",
   325→			provider: "unknown",
   326→			config:   map[string]string{"any_key": "any_value"},
   327→			wantErr:  false, // Unknown providers are allowed
   328→		},
   329→	}
   330→
   331→	for _, tt := range tests {
   332→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   333→			err := validator.validateProviderSpecificConfig(tt.provider, tt.config)
   334→			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
   335→				t.Errorf("validateProviderSpecificConfig() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   336→				return
   337→			}
   338→			if tt.wantErr && err != nil && tt.errMsg != "" {
   339→				if !containsString(err.Error(), tt.errMsg) {
   340→					t.Errorf("validateProviderSpecificConfig() error = %v, want error containing %v", err, tt.errMsg)
   341→				}
   342→			}
   343→		})
   344→	}
   345→}
   346→
   347→func TestLLMModelValidator_validateTokenLimits(t *testing.T) {
   348→	validator := NewLLMModelValidator()
   349→
   350→	tests := []struct {
   351→		name      string
   352→		maxTokens int
   353→		provider  string
   354→		wantErr   bool
   355→		errMsg    string
   356→	}{
   357→		{
   358→			name:      "valid openai tokens",
   359→			maxTokens: 128000,
   360→			provider:  "openai",
   361→			wantErr:   false,
   362→		},
   363→		{
   364→			name:      "too low tokens",
   365→			maxTokens: 500,
   366→			provider:  "openai",
   367→			wantErr:   true,
   368→			errMsg:    "max_tokens too low",
   369→		},
   370→		{
   371→			name:      "too high tokens general",
   372→			maxTokens: 3000000,
   373→			provider:  "openai",
   374→			wantErr:   true,
   375→			errMsg:    "max_tokens too high",
   376→		},
   377→		{
   378→			name:      "too high tokens for openai",
   379→			maxTokens: 1500000,
   380→			provider:  "openai",
   381→			wantErr:   true,
   382→			errMsg:    "max_tokens for OpenAI models cannot exceed",
   383→		},
   384→		{
   385→			name:      "too high tokens for anthropic",
   386→			maxTokens: 300000,
   387→			provider:  "anthropic",
   388→			wantErr:   true,
   389→			errMsg:    "max_tokens for Anthropic models cannot exceed",
   390→		},
   391→		{
   392→			name:      "high tokens for local",
   393→			maxTokens: 150000,
   394→			provider:  "local",
   395→			wantErr:   true,
   396→			errMsg:    "max_tokens for local models should not exceed",
   397→		},
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	for _, tt := range tests {
   401→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   402→			err := validator.validateTokenLimits(tt.maxTokens, tt.provider)
   403→			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
   404→				t.Errorf("validateTokenLimits() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   405→				return
   406→			}
   407→			if tt.wantErr && err != nil && tt.errMsg != "" {
   408→				if !containsString(err.Error(), tt.errMsg) {
   409→					t.Errorf("validateTokenLimits() error = %v, want error containing %v", err, tt.errMsg)
   410→				}
   411→			}
   412→		})
   413→	}
   414→}
   415→
   416→func TestValidateModelIDFormat(t *testing.T) {
   417→	tests := []struct {
   418→		name    string
   419→		id      string
   420→		wantErr bool
   421→		errMsg  string
   422→	}{
   423→		{
   424→			name:    "valid ID",
   425→			id:      "gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06",
   426→			wantErr: false,
   427→		},
   428→		{
   429→			name:    "valid simple ID",
   430→			id:      "claude-3-sonnet",
   431→			wantErr: false,
   432→		},
   433→		{
   434→			name:    "too short",
   435→			id:      "ab",
   436→			wantErr: true,
   437→			errMsg:  "model ID too short",
   438→		},
   439→		{
   440→			name:    "too long",
   441→			id:      "this-is-an-extremely-long-model-id-that-exceeds-the-maximum-allowed-length-for-model-identifiers",
   442→			wantErr: true,
   443→			errMsg:  "model ID too long",
   444→		},
   445→		{
   446→			name:    "invalid characters",
   447→			id:      "invalid$model#id",
   448→			wantErr: true,
   449→			errMsg:  "invalid characters",
   450→		},
   451→		{
   452→			name:    "starts with dash",
   453→			id:      "-invalid-model",
   454→			wantErr: true,
   455→			errMsg:  "cannot start or end with dash",
   456→		},
   457→		{
   458→			name:    "ends with underscore",
   459→			id:      "invalid-model_",
   460→			wantErr: true,
   461→			errMsg:  "cannot start or end with dash or underscore",
   462→		},
   463→	}
   464→
   465→	for _, tt := range tests {
   466→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   467→			err := ValidateModelIDFormat(tt.id)
   468→			if (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {
   469→				t.Errorf("ValidateModelIDFormat() error = %v, wantErr %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   470→				return
   471→			}
   472→			if tt.wantErr && err != nil && tt.errMsg != "" {
   473→				if !containsString(err.Error(), tt.errMsg) {
   474→					t.Errorf("ValidateModelIDFormat() error = %v, want error containing %v", err, tt.errMsg)
   475→				}
   476→			}
   477→		})
   478→	}
   479→}
   480→
   481→// Helper function to check if a string contains a substring
   482→func containsString(s, substr string) bool {
   483→	if len(substr) == 0 {
   484→		return true
   485→	}
   486→	if len(s) < len(substr) {
   487→		return false
   488→	}
   489→	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
   490→		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
   491→			return true
   492→		}
   493→	}
   494→	return false
   495→}
